item.title: Artemis II crew appear to be in high spirits ahead of moon mission
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Thousands lost to cancellations, expenses surge as fuel crisis bites southern Queensland tourism sector
item.description: Tourism businesses say they’re bearing the brunt of surging fuel prices, with some losing thousands to booking cancellations and extra operational costs, while others field back-to-back calls from anxious tourists about fuel availability.

item.title: Arsenal defeats Chelsea 3-2 on aggregate in Women's Champions League as Matildas face off
item.description: Steph Catley and Caitlin Foord come up against Matildas teammates Sam Kerr and Ellie Carpenter, as Arsenal prevails in its UEFA Women's Champions League quarterfinal against Chelsea.

item.title: How a youth outreach program is keeping teens engaged in Kalumburu
item.description: Kalumburu youth workers are giving teens the chance to get away from screens, be on country and connect with nature.

item.title: When work becomes your identity, is it time to nurture your non-work self?
item.description: The centrality of work in our lives can't be denied, especially when Australians are working longer hours than ever before. But short of quitting our jobs, how do we shift the balance?

item.title: Artemis II blasts off from launch pad
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Storm NRL forward Tui Kamikamica leaves hospital after suffering suspected stroke
item.description: The club says Tui Kamikamica will now "commence a period of inpatient rehabilitation" after the "medical episode" on Monday.

item.title: No Easter opening for WA's landmark Gloucester Tree climb with rebuild delayed
item.description: Plans to reopen the climb up the Gloucester Tree by the Easter long weekend have fallen by the wayside as work continues to make it safer for visitors.

item.title: Super Mario Galaxy Movie review: The only way out is through
item.description: Even with downgraded expectations, the Nintendo sequel is little more than Easter eggs and bright colours. But that won't stop it from filling cinemas.

item.title: Relocating tiger sharks may reduce short-term risk of shark bites, research suggests
item.description: A new study analysing the behaviour of tiger sharks relocated from the Great Barrier Reef Marine Park under the Queensland Shark Control Program shows tiger sharks relocated from swimming beaches generally return to a beach within about three months.

item.title: Crypto exchange platform markets in Australia despite ASIC intervention
item.description: A major cryptocurrency platform is operating like "gambling" in Australia, according to experts, despite new regulatory laws.

item.title: Powerful magnitude-7.4 earthquake strikes Indonesia, tsunami warning issued
item.description: The quake, which hit early today, had a depth of 35 kilometres and its epicentre was 127km west-north-west of Ternate, Indonesia, the US Geological Survey said.

item.title: More SA general practices charging extra administration fees to cover payroll tax
item.description: Patients are being asked to make an additional, separate transaction at general practices across Adelaide of between $5 and $10 to cover increased operating costs following the implementation of payroll tax for contracted doctors in 2024.

item.title: Australia to join 34 countries — but not the US — in meeting on Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Australia will join a virtual meeting of 35 countries to discuss plans to reopen and secure the Strait of Hormuz, and ease the oil shock rocking global markets.

item.title: States and territories agree to further fuel excise cut, using extra GST revenue
item.description: Premiers and chief ministers agree to use an estimated $400 million in extra GST revenue to fund a 5.7 per litre cut to the fuel excise, on top of the Commonwealth's 26.3 cent per litre relief.

item.title: Carlton's poor second halves are the talk of the AFL, so what is going wrong for the Blues?
item.description: Carlton's horrible second halves have become a costly trend which has the team struggling and Michael Voss fighting for his job. This is what is going wrong after half-time for the Blues.

item.title: In pictures: Artemis II NASA's first mission to the Moon in decades
item.description: For the first time in more than 50 years NASA has sent astronauts around the Moon, marking a major milestone in a new era of space exploration.

item.title: Albanese unveils long-awaited response to landmark gambling reform report
item.description: Gambling ads to be partially restricted on television and radio, with opt-out rules for online promotions and a ban in stadiums and on jerseys under reforms unveiled by Labor.

item.title: Australians continue to transit through Middle East despite 'do not travel' warnings
item.description: Australians are choosing cheaper airfares over safer flights to get to Europe by transiting through the Middle East, despite federal government warnings.

item.title: Essendon Bombers coach Brad Scott adamant club is not rebuilding despite losing streak
item.description: Essendon coach Brad Scott has a simple message for anyone who thinks his struggling AFL club is in a "rebuild" phase.

item.title: New Zealand security declaration with Cook Islands puts 'massive limitations' on China pacts, minister says
item.description: New Zealand and the Cook Islands have mended their relationship with a pact that restores funding to the Pacific island nation.

item.title: When is my child old enough to come running with me?
item.description: If you enjoy running and your child has shown interest in coming along, you might be wondering when they're old enough to start. Experts say there are a few ways you can determine if they're ready.

item.title: Your Good Friday fish and chips may be coming from as far away as South Africa this Easter
item.description: This Easter is the first in Western Australia under tough new demersal fishing rules. Seafood retailers say the change in supply and price is noticeable.

item.title: COVID vaccine injury victims speak out about slow claims process and threats over complaints made to Services Australia
item.description: For years these vaccine-injured Australians have been trying to get compensation from a 'torturous' government compensation scheme.

item.title: The PM gave the all-clear to 'enjoy your Easter'. Here's what you should know first about fuel
item.description: Thanks to fuel supply concerns your Easter trip may not look like normal. Here's what you need to know before hitting the road.

item.title: Sam Kerr in, Mary Fowler not part of Matildas squad for FIFA Series in Kenya
item.description: Sam Kerr has been named, but the Matildas will be missing a number of their other big guns when they travel to Kenya for the team's maiden FIFA Series in April.

item.title: 'Krill wars' back on in Southern Ocean as activist ship Bandero deliberately collides with Aker QRILL trawler
item.description: An environmental activist group’s ship has deliberately collided with a large fishing trawler in Antarctic waters. The activists say it was a "gentle nudge" but the trawler's owner says a line has been crossed.

item.title: NT police confirm external police review into Kumanjayi White death in custody complete
item.description: NT police have received the independent use of force review into the death in custody of Kumanjayi White in Alice Springs in 2025.

item.title: Albanese tries to get fuel message out as Chalmers's budget countdown begins
item.description: This week in Canberra, the prime minister had a message for Australians, his treasurer laid the groundwork for the upcoming federal budget and Angus Taylor's car was stolen.

item.title: One Nation wins its fourth seat in SA's lower house following Narungga recount
item.description: The seat of Narungga was so close it required a recount, and has now been won by One Nation candidate Chantelle Thomas by a margin of 58 votes over the Liberals.

item.title: Angus Taylor uses national address to call on Australians to 'look after ourselves'
item.description: Angus Taylor has issued a return serve to the prime minister's national address by urging people to "put Australia first" while accusing the government of providing a lack of clarity about the ongoing fuel crisis.

item.title: New recruits impress for Brisbane as Collingwood star Nick Daicos misses match due to corked calf
item.description: Collingwood is unsure whether Nick Daicos will spend time on the sidelines after the star midfielder was forced out of Thursday night's match with a calf injury.

item.title: Police officers swim out to rescue woman in Perth's Swan River
item.description: Two police officers who swam out into Perth's Swan River early on Thursday to help rescue a woman will be recommended for a bravery award.

item.title: Thursday finance with Daniel Ziffer
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Foran relieved after Manly's 'whirlwind week' ends with emphatic victory
item.description: Less than seven days after taking over from sacked coach Anthony Seibold, Kieran Foran takes immense pride in Manly's first win of the season.

item.title: Police decision to call off ambulance for Jeffrey Winmar scrutinised during custodial death inquest
item.description: The events leading up to the death of Indigenous father Jeffrey Winmar in police custody in Melbourne's east in 2023 are being examined through a coronial inquest.

item.title: Matildas' players join Japan criticising 'inequality' in prize money from Asian Football Confederation
item.description: Fierce rivals on the field, the Matildas and Japan have joined forces to slam the Asian Football Confederation's prize money for the Women's Asian Cup.

item.title: COVID vaccine claimants remain in administrative purgatory
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Discovering the depths of the Coral Sea
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Weekly news quiz: A lost penguin, successful NASA rocket launch and address to the nation
item.description: It's been a big week in the news. Did you catch these stories?

item.title: Cate Blanchett reunites with Jim Jarmusch on anthology movie Father Mother Sister Brother
item.description: The complexities of family light up Jim Jarmusch's Golden Lion-winner, Father Mother Sister Brother, which features Blanchett alongside Adam Driver and Tom Waits.

item.title: Price ceiling for rising oil prices comes with a caveat
item.description: Trying to guess exactly where the oil price tops out is fast becoming the chocolate wheel of choice among politicians, economists and oil experts.

item.title: What the shifting language of Australia's leaders reveals about the Iran war
item.description: With the Strait of Hormuz effectively closed, world oil markets rattled and no obvious off-ramp to the hostilities in sight, the major parties have dialled back their initial optimism.

item.title: Future fuel plan should be central to Albanese government's reform agenda
item.description: You could view the present contest between the Albanese government and the Taylor-Canavan opposition as pragmatism versus populism.

item.title: How the fuel crisis is hurting small regional Australian tourist towns like Foster
item.description: In one community, the effects of the fuel crisis are not always where you would think.

item.title: Increasing ACL injuries partly attributed to early specialisation in sport
item.description: Australia has one of the highest rates of ACL injuries per number of people in the world, and younger people have the highest chance of tearing the ligament.

item.title: Regional drivers shift gears to electric vehicles as fuel prices climb
item.description: EV uptake in regional Victoria is on the rise, but is everyone willing to make a switch?

item.title: Australia sources most of its jet fuel from Asia, but suppliers are feeling the pinch
item.description: Australia imports most of its jet fuel from Asia, particularly China, Singapore and South Korea. But those countries rely on the Middle East for crude oil, meaning they are particularly vulnerable to the impacts of war.

item.title: South Australian MP Jason Virgo's switch from the Australian Sex Party to One Nation
item.description: Sixteen years before Jason Virgo won the SA seat of MacKillop for One Nation in the state's south-east, he twice ran for parliament as an Australian Sex Party candidate.

item.title: What to know about fuel levies and surcharges before paying one
item.description: Shoppers may have noticed an extra charge at the checkout as businesses seek to offset rising operational costs associated with the fuel price hike. Here is what you need to know.

item.title: Fuel crisis highlights gaps in Western Sydney's public transport network
item.description: Mount Druitt residents would happily use public transport to avoid high fuel prices, but say it is not an option due to unreliable services.

item.title: Saints' Lance Collard to face tribunal for alleged homophobic slur in VFL match
item.description: St Kilda forward Lance Collard will front a disciplinary tribunal hearing next week after he allegedly used a homophobic slur during a VFL match.

item.title: Invasive feral hog deer discovered in New South Wales but authorities confident of eradication
item.description: Standing only 70 centimetres tall, the hog deer has not been seen in the state for more than a decade, but poses a significant environmental threat.

item.title: Bret McKenzie on Flight of the Conchords reunion, Oscar win and songs without jokes
item.description: From Flight of the Conchords to Oscar-winning film and TV songwriting, Bret McKenzie always finds the fun in his career.

item.title: Fuel crisis driving some Tasmanian farmers to consider abandoning harvests
item.description: Tasmanian farmers are considering not harvesting crops like broccoli and cauliflower because increasing freight costs are proving to be so debilitating.

item.title: Matildas captain Sam Kerr shoots down Chelsea exit rumour after report of potential US move
item.description: Matildas captain Sam Kerr is quick to cast doubt on a report she will leave Chelsea at the end of the season to join US club Denver Summit.

item.title: SA fishers demand urgent action to protect stocks, fearing long-term wipeout
item.description: Easter is a popular time for consumers to purchase fish, but commercial fishers in South Australia say stocks have been decimated by the algal bloom. They fear it will get worse without further action.

item.title: WA holiday-makers stick closer to home over fuel supply fears
item.description: Accommodation providers in regional WA tourism hotspots see a high number of cancellations, as holiday-makers choose to stay close to home due to fuel prices.

item.title: Cameron Green's IPL woes for Knight Riders continue as Travis Head fires for Sunrisers
item.description: Cameron Green is unable to arrest his shaky start to the Indian Premier League season, but Travis Head blasts back to his best.

item.title: 'Rare parasitic fungus' behind outbreak of severe conjunctivitis in Top End wet season footballers
item.description: Health authorities say a rare water-borne pathogen is behind the recent outbreak of severe conjunctivitis in Top End football clubs, with a number of Darwin playing fields deemed high-risk infection sites.

item.title: What to watch and listen to this weekend: Aaron Chen, Super Mario Galaxy and Robyn
item.description: Break out the chocolate, pick up the remote: we've got you covered for Easter weekend.

item.title: Cafe owners calls for visa changes to stop backpackers leaving WA's holiday hotspots
item.description: Visa rules allow backpackers to conduct "remote work" on the island, just 33km from Perth's city centre, but not in WA's South West — something business owners want to change.

item.title: Fuel crisis, cyclones, a teen hero — here are the photos that tell WA's stories in February and March
item.description: Here are some of our top photo picks from the past two months of news coverage.

item.title: Port Macquarie family travels overseas for unproven stem-cell treatment as experts urge caution
item.description: A young mother who travelled overseas for controversial stem cell therapy hopes new treatments will one day be available in Australia, but experts warn there's still a "long way" to go.

item.title: Australia scores nine-wicket win over West Indies in final ODI in St Kitts
item.description: Alana King stars with the ball, as the Australian women's team finish their white-ball tour of West Indies with a resounding nine-wicket win in St Kitts.

item.title: US negotiator in 2015 Iran nuclear deal says Donald Trump 'delusional' on nuclear and regime change
item.description: Robert Malley, who negotiated the 2015 nuclear deal with Iran, says Donald Trump's address to the US this week was designed to deceive.

item.title: Fitzroy River plan sparks concern among pastoralists and traditional owners
item.description: A long-awaited draft plan aimed at protecting one of Western Australia's largest rivers has been released for public consultation, but traditional owners, environmental groups and pastoralists say key changes are needed.

item.title: SA Electoral Commission apologises to staff over pay delay following state election
item.description: The Electoral Commission of South Australia has apologised to staff who have not been fully paid almost a fortnight after the state election, saying a payroll processing delay had caused the problem.

item.title: NASA Artemis II astronauts rocket towards Moon after spending day orbiting Earth
item.description: The Orion capsule carrying four astronauts in NASA's Artemis II mission successfully fired its engines this morning, kicking the crew out of Earth's orbit and bound for the Moon.

item.title: FIFA raises top ticket price for World Cup final to nearly $16,000 during glitch-hampered sales reopening
item.description: The top ticket price was $15,900, a sharp increase from the original $12,560 when FIFA sold tickets after the tournament draw in December.

item.title: Dockers claim thriller after Crow hits post inside final minute
item.description: Fremantle coughs up a five-goal lead before holding on in the final quarter after Adelaide's James Borlase hits the post with 30 seconds left on the clock.

item.title: Westpac CEO warns 'there's a chance of a recession' as interest rates rise
item.description: Westpac's chief executive, Anthony Miller, says Australians need to acknowledge there is a growing risk the country could slip into recession, amid the Iran war-driven fuel crisis and rising interest rates.

item.title: Bellamy threatens to wield axe after Storm's humiliating loss to Panthers
item.description: Melbourne Storm coach Craig Bellamy puts his players on notice after they were thrashed 50-10 by Penrith on Good Friday.

item.title: Keeping Trump and his MAGA base happy proved too tough for Pam Bondi
item.description: Pam Bondi and Kristi Noem were both fired after controversial tenures carrying out Donald Trump's instructions in two of the trickiest roles in his administration.

item.title: National Institute of Dramatic Art's former director John Clark dies aged 93
item.description: A giant of Australian theatre who helped launch the careers of some of the country's biggest names of the stage has died aged 93.

item.title: Government says billions of litres of fuel are en route to Australia, but industry stakeholders aren't so optimistic
item.description: Those in trucking industry are not getting hopes up that fuel will arrive in Australia, after Energy Minister announces 53 ships are on their way.

item.title: World loses patience with Trump as war no longer just about Iran
item.description: Global leaders are giving up the pretence of diplomatic niceties as they lose patience with the US president's actions.

item.title: How Gordi transformed a song meant for Alex Lahey into her stunning new single
item.description: Gordi's new single was originally meant to be for her partner. Thankfully, both artists know the importance of trusting their gut.

item.title: Best podcasts to listen to this Easter long weekend, from Expanse: The Nannup Four to Empire City
item.description: The podcasts we're listening to this weekend include an investigation into the Salt Path scandal and a personal look at the role of Australia in the end of apartheid.

item.title: Soaring gas prices from Iran war push Southeast Asian countries to the brink
item.description: Countries heavily reliant on imported oil, like the Philippines in Southeast Asia, are most vulnerable to the unfolding energy crisis caused by the US-Israel war with Iran.

item.title: Sailors trapped by Iran's Strait of Hormuz blockade plead for help
item.description: Interviews and first-hand accounts from sailors stranded in the Persian Gulf — under threat of drone attacks, missiles, and exploding boats — reveal an increasingly dire humanitarian crisis unfolding.

item.title: How Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle wreaked havoc across QLD, WA and the NT
item.description: From the very first news reports about Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle, it was clear this wasn't just another storm. This is the story of how the cyclone impacted the entire span of northern Australia and how communities came together to pick up the pieces.

item.title: Strategically critical Guam divided over growing US military presence
item.description: The Pacific Island of Guam sits at the centre of a struggle between the world's most powerful military and the right of its people to determine their own future.

item.title: Thinking of changing your private health insurance? Ask these five questions
item.description: The loopholes to look for if you're thinking about downgrading or changing your private health insurance.

item.title: One Nation leaves SA Liberals clinging to opposition status
item.description: The Liberal Party will have just one more seat than One Nation in South Australia’s lower house, leaving opposition leader Ashton Hurn little margin for error.

item.title: Australian grey-headed flying fox economic and ecological value of poo revealed
item.description: Bats are often thought of as smelly, ugly and noisy, but a study has revealed the economic and ecological value of one Australian species's poo.

item.title: Blues AFL coach Michael Voss unfazed about speculation over his future after Kangaroos loss
item.description: Michael Voss knows he faces more scrutiny over his role as Blues coach after his side endures another second-half meltdown.

item.title: Long-term travellers camp out or alter plans due to surging fuel prices
item.description: Surging diesel costs have caught many travellers off guard, forcing them to camp out, cancel, or push on and wear the hit to their hip pockets.

item.title: Avocado 'ripeness scanner' trial aims to help customers choose the perfect fruit
item.description: An Australian-first avocado "ripeness scanner" is being trialled, with the aim of telling customers exactly when their avocados will be ready to eat.

item.title: This Easter my heart is with the casualties and refugees in Lebanon
item.description: Lebanese families have been told to flee homes that they might never be able to return to, and families are setting up tents for an uncertain and dangerous life on the streets of Beirut all because of a conflict not of their making, brought about by another state.

item.title: Art created to help Papunya children read in their language goes on display at National Library of Australia
item.description: More than 40 years ago, a program was created to help Papunya children in remote NT learn to read in Pintupi-Luritja languages. Those books are now an insight into the people, stories, history and culture of the Papunya and are on display at the National Library of Australia.

item.title: FluMist nasal spray vaccine is available for use in Australia, but only some children will get it for free
item.description: It's been available in the northern hemisphere for years, now Australian children can receive a nasal spray vaccine for the flu.

item.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: This week we're testing your knowledge of Top Gun, bowling underarm and your weight on the Moon
item.description: Every question has only two options: one is right and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

item.title: Iran's downing of two US aircraft delivers President Donald Trump's quagmire moment
item.description: This is the point when the US either goes deeper into this war or makes a clear decision to end it.

item.title: Why 18-year-old Daniel Williams is among the rising stars of Australian athletics
item.description: Teenagers Gout Gout and Cameron Myers are already creating an impact on the international stage, helping to inspire the likes of 18-year-old Daniel Williams to follow in their footsteps.

item.title: Interest in novated leases for electric vehicles soaring in Australia amid fuel crisis
item.description: With the petrol bowser continuing to hurt wallets, more Australians than ever are considering getting an electric vehicle through a novated lease.

item.title: Outback Queensland's Nindigully Pub changes hands after 24 years
item.description: After turning "the worst pub you ever walked into" into a tourism award winner, Stephen Burns is stepping away from managing the Nindigully Pub.

item.title: Demons star Kysaiah Pickett praised after brilliant start to AFL season
item.description: Ed Langdon says Melbourne teammate Kysaiah Pickett is flourishing in his midfield role, as the Demons continue life under new coach Steven King.

item.title: How often should you change your toilet brush?
item.description: If you're just shoving the brush into its holder to wait for the next use and actively avoiding thinking about how dirty the whole concept of it is, you're not alone. Here's how to make sure your toilet brush is as hygienic as possible while it lasts.

item.title: Artemis II's Moon-bound NASA astronauts capture Earth's blue beauty
item.description: As Artemis II astronauts forge ahead, NASA has released new images from inside the Orion spacecraft, including a portrait of Earth's deep blue oceans.

item.title: Farmer and First Nations artist collaborate to create beautiful, cultural fashion
item.description: When a farmer and a First Nations artist join forces, something beautiful emerges — thanks to Japanese knitting technology.

item.title: Artemis II crew make video contact from space
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Local governments support move to add world-famous Bibbulmun Track to heritage list
item.description: Councils support moves for the Bibbulmun Track to be declared a heritage-listed site, which conservationists hope could protect the track from future mining expansions.

item.title: Ricky Stuart not worried about Raiders' poor start to NRL season ahead of Knights clash
item.description: The Raiders have triumphed in only one of their opening four matches, but coach Ricky Stuart says the club has been in "worse positions than this".

item.title: Arnhem Land rock art raises questions about Tasmanian tigers living on mainland
item.description: Newly documented rock art in Arnhem Land has raised questions about how long Tasmanian tigers lived on the mainland before they were wiped out.

item.title: Djalama traditional owner Joey Nganjmirra explains how he identified a painting as depicting a thylacine.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Demand for fuel remains high as farmers urge supermarkets to pay more for fresh produce
item.description: The effects of Australia's fuel shortage begin to compound for farmers, who have called on supermarkets to help relieve the pressure, while the government urges consumers not to panic-buy.

item.title: Eagles back Reid after standing up for teammate in heavy loss to Swans
item.description: Eagles coach Andrew McQualter leaps to Harley Reid's defence after his discipline came into question during the 128-point thrashing dished out by the Swans.

item.title: Richardson flies past Stawell Gift opponents
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Andrew Lethlean to challenge Jacinta Allan in Bendigo East
item.description: Publican Andrew Lethlean will run as the Nationals candidate for the seat of Bendigo East in the upcoming state election.

item.title: Police launch large-scale search to locate Perth woman missing in popular tourism spot
item.description: A large-scale search is underway for 51-year-old Naomi Marie Duckett, who was last seen at Gap Natural Bridge within Torndirrup National Park just before midday on Thursday.

item.title: Walsh hospitalised following Broncos’ controversial win
item.description: Brisbane star Reece Walsh is taken to hospital after the Broncos record a controversial 26-12 win over Gold Coast.

item.title: Sha'Carri Richardson, Christian Coleman win Stawell Gift heats to reach Easter Monday semifinals
item.description: US sprint couple Sha'Carri Richardson and Christian Coleman win their Stawell Gift heats, with a drone shot showing Richardson's dominance from the back marker.

item.title: Albanese's big gamble on betting industry restrictions
item.description: Labor's landslide re-election brought renewed hope of reform, but in the past 12 months many anti-gambling advocates only became further disillusioned.

item.title: Thom Yorke Live at Sydney Opera House is made by fans, for fans
item.description: The award-winning Australian team behind a new concert film of the Radiohead frontman's debut solo tour discusses how it happened and why it matters.

item.title: Hard Quiz: Hurry up, I don't have all day to watch you struggle at internet trivia
item.description: It's painfully obvious you're going to fail this quiz, so let's get it over with and return to our Sunday mornings, shall we?

item.title: Queen's 100th birthday fashion exhibition unfolds, Andrew questions linger
item.description: Within palace walls the Queen was a godlike figure. As her glorious fashion goes on display to commemorate what would have been her 100th birthday, how would she have responded to the Andrew crisis if alive today?

item.title: Alleged cocaine smugglers' three-month journey from Panama to Sydney ends in arrest
item.description: Setting off from Panama just before Christmas, police say the MV Raider started its journey towards Sydney with almost 6 tonnes of cocaine aboard worth nearly $2 billion.

item.title: High fuel prices mean some young Australians cannot afford to get to work
item.description: Young people are taking extraordinary steps to deal with the fuel crisis, including cutting back on work because the cost of fuel to get there is now higher than the money they will earn for the shift.

item.title: Stagflation 'is happening' says a leading economist. What can be done about it?
item.description: With stagflation risks rising, what lessons could the treasurer learn from the past?

item.title: Children with assault rifles in uniform paraded through Iran
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Kids with guns and preparing for a ground invasion. What people in Iran say is happening
item.description: Kids as young as 12 are being given guns in Iran, as Donald Trump threatens a ground invasion. Here's what people in Iran are feeling.

item.title: Deforestation and empty promises turn violent storms into deadly disasters
item.description: Months after floods killed more than a thousand people across Sumatra, residents are still digging their way out of the wreckage. While survivors are eager to rebuild, many just can’t shake the fear that the next catastrophe is just around the corner.

item.title: Detainees from Cobham Youth Justice Centre read their letter to the prime minister.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: New research shows regional Australians move on foot more in 'walkable' towns
item.description: The humble footpath could be the answer to being more active, with new research showing regional Australians living in "walkable towns" travelled 75 minutes more on foot each week.

item.title: Aerial photographer's hunt for salt lake 'Easter eggs' yields striking images in WA
item.description: Scott McCook spends his spare time photographing the salt lakes of WA's Wheatbelt, which look uncannily like giant eggs when viewed from the air.

item.title: The 'mudcrabs' volunteering to remediate Sydney's Cooks River from toxic pollution
item.description: Scurrying about the banks of Sydney's Cooks River, an army of volunteers known as the 'mudcrabs' is working to bring one of the nation's most toxic waterways back to life.

item.title: Mount Gambier's 'Blue Blob' sculpture sparked controversy at first. How do locals feel about it now?
item.description: Cast in Blue has been the subject of criticism, ridicule and even vandalism over the past 10 months, but Mount Gambier's community has begun to embrace it.

item.title: Fuel crisis forces some motorists to change commuting habits
item.description: A growing number of Australians are factoring rising fuel costs into a tightening budget, including seeking cheaper alternative transport, with one charity warning the flow on effects of the crisis are causing household stress for many families.

item.title: Zac Lomax's Super Rugby Pacific debut delayed as Force defeat Reds 42-19 in Brisbane
item.description: Injury delays Zac Lomax's professional rugby debut, but the Force may have no choice but to throw their star recruit in the deep end when they next face Fijian Drua.

item.title: Restoration begins on historic Pride of the Murray paddleboat in Longreach
item.description: A century-old wooden paddleboat sitting on the banks of an outback river has quite the story to tell, and a restoration will ensure its journey continues.

item.title: Two artists who created remarkably similar works 50 years apart celebrated at National Portrait Gallery
item.description: The new exhibition at the National Portrait Gallery brings together the work of two Australian artists working nearly half a century apart, and asks visitors to find the sparks connecting them.

item.title: Brazil's Luana Silva elated after beating Stephanie Gilmore at Bells Beach
item.description: Luana Silva is overwhelmed to defeat one of her idols, Australia's eight-time world champion Stephanie Gilmore, in the first round.

item.title: A regent honeyeater spotted in northern NSW is recorded mimicking the call of a little wattlebird
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Conservationists successfully teach young regent honeyeaters how to sing their unique song
item.description: There are only about 250 regent honeyeaters left in the wild, and they have been losing their unique call, mimicking other birds instead. Conservationists say they are now successfully teaching young birds their traditional song.

item.title: Australia's Annie Anezakis helps Oxford defeat Cambridge in women's Boat Race
item.description: Competing for Oxford in the historic women's Boat Race for a fourth time, Australia's Annie Anezakis finally tastes victory.

item.title: Albany woman smashes WA's state record for heaviest pumpkin with 408kg feat
item.description: A pumpkin grown in Albany has beaten the previous WA record for heaviest pumpkin by nearly 60 kilograms.

item.title: Artemis astronauts preparing for historic lunar flyby
item.description: The Artemis astronauts have been taking a moment to look back at Earth as they continue deep into space toward the Moon.

item.title: $2.2 million funding boost to crack down on fuel price hikes in NSW
item.description: The NSW government fast-tracks a compliance blitz to strengthen fuel price transparency and protect motorists from misleading pricing.

item.title: Reece Walsh out for up to six weeks with fractured cheekbone after Broncos' NRL win over Titans
item.description: The Brisbane Broncos are dealt a massive blow in their NRL premiership defence, with Reece Walsh set for a lengthy stint away from the game.

item.title: All regional retailers ordered to join FuelWatch reporting scheme as WA government tackles ongoing oil shock
item.description: The FuelWatch reporting scheme is being expanded to all regional fuel stations in Western Australia, with about 200 additional retailers required to report their fuel prices.

item.title: Artemis II crew work inside space capsule on historic lunar mission
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Magnitude-5.5 earthquake recorded in APY Lands near Northern Territory and South Australian border
item.description: A remote community in the Anangu Pitjantjatjara Yankunytjatjara (APY) Lands have woken up to rattling shelves and swinging lights caused by a magnitude-5.5 earthquake, Geoscience Australia says.

item.title: North West Coastal Highway reopens after damage from ex-Cyclone Narelle
item.description: Holiday makers took a gamble heading to WA's north hoping a major highway would reopen in time for Easter.

item.title: Rescuers save huge sea turtle discovered stranded upside down on Coffs Coast beach
item.description: A large sea turtle, estimated to be at least 30 years old and weighing more than 50 kilograms, is recovering in a wildlife sanctuary after being spotted stuck in dunes on a northern NSW beach.

item.title: Kings break NBL trophy after dramatic overtime win as 36ers blow late lead
item.description: After trailing by six points late in regulation time, a dominant overtime display has earned the Sydney Kings a 113-101 victory in the fifth and deciding match of the NBL Finals.

item.title: Can Australia afford free childcare?
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Raiders' season lurches towards disaster as Knights run riot
item.description: The reigning minor premiers are in freefall after Canberra slumped to a fourth-straight loss at the hands of a resurgent Knights outfit.

item.title: Lily Mora explores origins of Australian modernism in new show at Heide Museum of Modern Art
item.description: A new show at the Heide Museum of Modern Art explores how the famous Heide Circle reimagined the family in radical ways in the 50s and 60s.

item.title: Vladimir Putin's burgeoning crackdown on Russia's internet is a 'throwback to the Soviet era', experts say
item.description: The Kremlin is increasing its efforts to dictate the narrative in Russia by shutting off the internet, restricting foreign messenger apps and punishing citizens for speaking out.

item.title: Australia given fuel supply assurances as plans in works for visit by Japan's prime minister
item.description: Fuel will continue to flow from Japan into Australia under assurances given during recent talks between the two countries.

item.title: Bulldogs down plucky Bombers but lose two more players to injury
item.description: The Western Bulldogs hold off an Essendon fight back to triumph by 34 points, but will sweat on the fitness of Artie Jones and Tim English, who both limped off at Docklands.

item.title: US airman rescued in Iran after special forces operation
item.description: US President Donald Trump confirms an American airman has been rescued in a daring night operation by US special forces.

item.title: Fuel costs hit some farmers hard, forces families to change holiday travel plans
item.description: Soaring fuel costs and interest rates are forcing some South Australians to change their travel plans as a small producer says he has struggled to put food on the table.

item.title: Fiji cruise ship runs aground after hitting reef near 'Cast Away island'
item.description: Thirty passengers have reportedly been rescued after a cruise ship ran aground on a reef near the island where the film Cast Away was shot.

item.title: Tim Tszyu dominates Denis Nurja to claim international middleweight title in Wollongong
item.description: Tim Tszyu has won the WBO international middleweight title with a lopsided unanimous points win over Albanian Denis Nurja in Wollongong.

item.title: This week in history quiz, April 6: Take a walk down memory lane
item.description: Where was the Venus de Milo found? And what common household item first went on sale in April 1827?

item.title: A great gift or unexpected curse? Reflections on life as a new retiree
item.description: While time is retirement's great gift, it can become a curse for anyone who has devoted decades to a career and then suddenly wakes up with relevance deprivation syndrome and no firm plan on what comes next, writes Matt Eaton.

item.title: After 60 years, witnesses to Australia's biggest UFO sighting at Westall High School say it's time for answers
item.description: Flying saucers, balloons or mass hysteria? It's 60 years today since Australia's biggest UFO sighting took place at a Melbourne school. Witnesses are still demanding answers about what happened that day.

item.title: As Iran charges for access to Strait of Hormuz, world faces dangerous new era of maritime tollbooths
item.description: If Iran's idea of charging ships to pass through maritime choke points catches on, we are entering a new era of much more expensive, more dangerous global shipping.

item.title: Chinese satellite imagery of Middle East bases is helping Iran, US intelligence says
item.description: A Chinese company's publication of AI-enhanced satellite imagery of US bases in the Middle East is helping Iranian forces identify targets, US intelligence believes.

item.title: Lebanese victim describes 45-minute Israeli attack in country's south, where Christian villages are under threat
item.description: Bashir was injured when Israel attacked his home in a Christian village in Lebanon's south, where many residents refuse to leave despite threats of destruction.

item.title: Interview with CSIRO scientist Melita Keywood at Kennaook/Cape Grim
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Drone view of Kennaook/Cape Grim
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Cape Grim's 50-year air record reveals true scale of humans' impact on planet
item.description: Perched on Tasmania's wild north-west coast, Cape Grim has been quietly tracking the world's cleanest air for 50 years. Its data tell an interesting story about how our world has changed over half a century, including rare success stories.

item.title: Petrol station workers abused and in-store profits fall as operators deal with Middle East fuel crisis
item.description: Workers are reporting an increase in abuse from customers as service station owners say they're not making money from fuel price rises.

item.title: Tree proximity to property may hurt sale price, analysis in Australia's biggest city shows
item.description: Tree canopy coverage is being boosted in every capital city, but one micro-level analysis has found a link between property prices and the proximity of trees to houses.

item.title: Zac Lomax's false start with Force adds to anticipation ahead of Super Rugby Pacific debut
item.description: Fans will have to wait for Zac Lomax's Western Force debut, providing further intrigue about how the ex-NRL star will cope when he takes his first steps in professional rugby.

item.title: How clean should a rental property be before moving in?
item.description: Having to clean a new rental property before settling in is a common experience for renters, but landlords have obligations when it comes to rental cleanliness upon moving in.

item.title: Terminal diagnosis hasn't stopped the final exhibition for Joan Humble
item.description: After a lifetime of capturing Tasmanian landscapes, Joan Humble is holding her final exhibition. The 88-year-old has a terminal cancer diagnosis, but she is determined to keep painting as long as she can hold a brush.

item.title: Dance classes providing connection, creativity and movement for older Australians
item.description: Judy Taylor discovered dance later in life, but it has now become a source of joy, energy, and purpose.

item.title: Showers for Queensland east coast as forecasters watch Tropical Cyclone Maila
item.description: The majority of Queensland will enjoy mild weather this week, with scattered showers across the east coast, while further north, Cyclone Maila is set to intensify.

item.title: Geelong's Corio Bowls skate park earmarked for heritage protection
item.description: A set of skate bowls with origins as an outlet for teens in a tough Geelong suburb, once visited by Tony Hawk, may soon be preserved long-term through heritage protection.

item.title: Live: Iran warns of 'devastating' retaliation after Trump vows to 'blow up whole country'
item.description: Tehran is warning of "much more devastating" retaliation after US President Donald Trump's expletive-laden outburst on social media called on Iran once again to open the Strait of Hormuz. Follow live.

item.title: Iran briefing with Matthew Doran: Threats tell us more about Trump's frustration than anything else
item.description: It's day 37 of the war in the Middle East. The ABC's Middle East correspondent Matthew Doran is in Jerusalem, where Donald Trump's threats to obliterate Iran are starting to feel like business as usual.

item.title: Easter tourism brings hope of economic recovery for fire-hit communities in Victoria
item.description: Businesses in fire-affected communities of Victoria welcome holiday-makers and daytrippers, as the recovery continues.

item.title: Youth detention watchdog calls for upgrade to 'harmful' cells to improve children's access to justice
item.description: South Australia's Youth Court cells do not meet "acceptable minimum standards of safety, dignity and care", resulting in child detainees increasingly appearing in court via video link, the state's youth detention watchdog says.

item.title: Jason Day battling irons ahead of Masters tilt after career of near misses
item.description: After five top-10 finishes at the Masters, including last year, Australian Jason Day returns to Augusta in hot form as he wrestles with his clubs.

item.title: Angry customers who lost millions in Zone RV collapse demand ASIC investigation
item.description: With retirement dreams dashed, customers launch a nationwide campaign for the corporate watchdog to investigate the caravan company's former director over insolvent trading claims.

item.title: Commuters swap the car for e-bikes as fuel prices soar amid Middle East war
item.description: As high fuel prices continue to affect motorists, Australians are turning to e-bikes as an alternative form of transport, with retailers reporting a surge in sales.

item.title: Hundreds of flying fox pups starving to death in wake of ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle
item.description: Rescuers are scrambling to save as many flying fox pups as they can, with hundreds of the native bats dead or dying in Top End streets.

item.title: 'Easter bilby' populations quadruple across Australian sanctuaries
item.description: The Australian Wildlife Conservancy says the threatened species has rebounded at multiple sites across the country.

item.title: Feral animals, biosecurity and careers on agenda for SA Aboriginal Land and Sea Rangers
item.description: The first SA Aboriginal Land and Sea Ranger Conference has brought together those on the front line of the fight against feral animals and plants.

item.title: Zendaya and Robert Pattinson star in new film The Drama
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Tim David stars in Royal Challengers Bengaluru's win over Chennai Super Kings
item.description: It is a mixed day for the Australians in the Indian Premier League, with Tim David powering RCB to a victory, while Travis Head fails to make an impact in Sunrisers Hyderabad's loss in Lucknow.

item.title: Moira Deeming survives Victorian Liberal preselection battle after rivals withdraw
item.description: The outspoken MP has officially won top spot on the party's upper-house ticket for the Western Metropolitan Region after other candidates withdrew.

item.title: Up to five on the run after ramraiding Brisbane CBD Hermès store
item.description: Police say a Mercedes SUV was deliberately driven into the store in the early hours today.

item.title: From Sway to Red Sunset: Bic Runga's return to music reflects where she's at
item.description: "There were definitely times where I thought I was never going to make anything again," the New Zealand star says of new album Red Sunset.

item.title: Train splits Tour of Flanders as Tadej Pogačar wins for third time, Demi Vollering wins women's race
item.description: Cycling great Tadej Pogačar wins the one-day classic Tour of Flanders for a record-equalling third time, but only after a controversial train crossing splits the field.

item.title: Push to axe Kanye West as headliner for London's Wireless Festival after antisemitic remarks
item.description: There is increasing pressure for the rapper to be pulled as headliner of the Wireless Festival in London, as UK PM Keir Starmer weighs in and Pepsi withdraws as lead sponsor.

item.title: Body found after house fire in Devonport, in north-west Tasmania
item.description: A woman in her 60s is believed to be the victim of a fatal house fire in Tasmania's north-west early this morning, firefighters say.

item.title: Search for 65yo swept away trying to cross floodwater on foot in SA's far north
item.description: Police are searching for a man in his 60s who was swept away trying to cross a flooded road on foot in SA's far north over the Easter weekend.

item.title: Sydney Royal Easter Show helps emerging artists build their careers
item.description: Horse drawings and handcrafted ceramics from local artists and artisans are among the arts and crafts you can find at the Sydney Royal Easter Show.

item.title: Australia's fuel shipments secured 'well into' May, energy minister says
item.description: Fuel shipments to Australia have been secured "well into" May, Energy Minister Chris Bowen says, as the number of service stations with diesel and petrol shortages fell over the Easter weekend.

item.title: Sprint superstar Sha'Carri Richardson reaches Stawell Gift final after close finish in semifinal
item.description: American 100-metre world champion and Olympic silver medallist Sha'Carri Richardson wins her Stawell Gift semifinal by just 0.007 of a second to make it through to this afternoon's final.

item.title: Artist shares origin story of Field of Light installation at Uluru
item.description: Bruce Munro's art installation is inspired by Uluru. Ten years later, it remains one of the most popular attractions at the famous landmark.

item.title: 16yo surfer says he feels 'lucky' to have his foot after shark attack at Middleton Beach
item.description: Oliver Tokic-Bensley says he was sitting on his surfboard alone at Middleton Beach when he said he felt something grab his foot. Warning: This story contains images some readers may find distressing.

item.title: Mayhem on the Murray as Mildura 100 speed boat racing puts four in hospital
item.description: Flips, spin-outs and crashes have sent four competitors to hospital as the world's fastest water ski race was held without skiers for the second straight year after fatalities in the sport in 2024.

item.title: Murray River race boat crashes up the bank and into a gum tree.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Artemis II crew about to smash record for the furthest humans have ventured from Earth
item.description: The Apollo 13 crew has held the distance record for a manned aircraft's maximum range from Earth since the 1970s. The Artemis II crew is about to change that.
